intro: Postmark enters at $15/mo (10K emails) against Lob at $260/mo. Both tools position themselves for transactional emails (receipts, alerts), so the decision rarely comes down to surface features. In practice, Lob — Messaging API (Email / SMS / Voice) tool for developers. Specializes in Direct Mail API. Postmark — Fast transactional email delivery API focused on inbox placement with detailed bounce analytics. Lob carries medium lock-in (Direct mail API) while Postmark is low (Low — REST API, SMTP). Postmark rates 5/5 on transparency versus 4/5 for Lob. Listed strengths unique to Lob include multi-channel delivery (sms, email, voice) from one api, while Postmark lists low lock-in — easy to migrate away on its side. Both tools surface overlapping when-to-use scenarios, including transactional emails (receipts, alerts). The honest trade-off: Lob is not built for internal projects, and Postmark is not the right call for internal projects, so neither is a universal default.
quick_take: Lob is for transactional emails (receipts, alerts); Postmark overlaps on the same job; decide based on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Lob if transactional emails (receipts, alerts) and the $260/mo entry price fits your budget.
verdict_b: Choose Postmark if transactional emails (receipts, alerts) and the $15/mo (10K emails) entry price fits your budget.

faq.a: Based on the listed entry prices, no: Lob starts at $260/mo while Postmark starts at $15/mo (10K emails). At low volume the gap may be small, but it widens fast at scale. Always price against your own usage curve, not the headline tier.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Lob to Postmark?
faq.a: Technically you can move between any two tools in the same category, but effort varies: leaving Lob is moderate effort at medium lock-in (Direct mail API), and onboarding to Postmark should be straightforward given its low lock-in. Plan for data export, re-integration, and a parallel-run window rather than a hard cutover.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, Lob or Postmark?
faq.a: On the 0-5 developer experience score in our data, Postmark rates 5/5 while Lob rates 4/5, giving Postmark the edge. That score is a rough summary; actual day-to-day DX depends on your language, deployment target, and tolerance for edge cases. Prototype both to validate.

faq.q: Is Postmark a good alternative to Lob?
faq.a: Yes, Postmark targets the same job Lob does — transactional emails (receipts, alerts) — so functionally it slots in as an alternative. Pricing model (usage from $15/mo (10K emails) vs subscription from $260/mo) and lock-in (low lock-in (Low — REST API, SMTP) vs medium lock-in (Direct mail API)) usually decide which is the better fit. Validate on your actual workload. Note: Postmark is explicitly not for internal projects.
